lines: // TDD Test Runner for CSV Report Generator
-// This file contains both the library code and the test suite. In .NET 10
-// file-based apps, top-level statements run first, and type declarations
-// (records, classes) go at the bottom of the file.
+//   2. Write minimum code to make it pass (GREEN)
-// TDD cycle: tests were conceptually written FIRST to define the API contract,
-// then the implementation was written to satisfy them (red → green → refactor).
-void AssertEqual<T>(T expected, T actual, string message)
+void AssertEqual<T>(T expected, T actual, string label)
-void AssertApprox(decimal expected, decimal actual, string message, decimal tolerance = 0.01m)
+void AssertApprox(decimal expected, decimal actual, string label, decimal tol = 0.01m)
+void AssertThrows<TEx>(Action action, string label) where TEx : Exception
+Console.WriteLine("\n═══ CSV Report Generator — TDD Test Suite ═══\n");
-// Test: Parse a single CSV row
+// Test: Parse a single CSV row into an Employee record
-    AssertEqual("Alice", employees[0].Name, "Parse single row: name = Alice");
-    AssertEqual("Engineering", employees[0].Department, "Parse single row: department = Engineering");
-    AssertEqual(new DateTime(2020, 1, 1), employees[0].HireDate, "Parse single row: hire_date");
-    AssertEqual("active", employees[0].Status, "Parse single row: status = active");
+    AssertEqual(new DateTime(2020, 1, 15), employees[0].HireDate, "Parse single row: hire_date");
-    AssertEqual("A", employees[0].Name, "Parse multiple rows: first = A");
-    AssertEqual("B", employees[1].Name, "Parse multiple rows: second = B");
+    AssertEqual("A", employees[0].Name, "Parse multiple rows: first name");
+    AssertEqual("B", employees[1].Name, "Parse multiple rows: second name");
-// Test: Header-only CSV returns empty list
-// Test: Empty string returns empty list
// Test: Too few columns throws FormatException
-    AssertTrue(threw, "Parse too few columns: throws FormatException");
-// Test: Filter keeps only active employees
-    AssertEqual(0, active.Count, "ActiveOnly: returns 0 when all inactive");
-    AssertEqual(2, active.Count, "ActiveOnly: keeps all when all active");
+    AssertEqual(2, EmployeeFilter.ActiveOnly(all).Count, "ActiveOnly: keeps all when all active");
-// Test: OverallStats on empty list throws
+// Test: OverallStats throws on empty collection
var single = new List<Employee> { new("Solo", "IT", 75000m, DateTime.Now, "active") };
-    AssertTrue(report.Contains("90,000.00") || report.Contains("90000"), "Report: contains avg salary for Eng");
+    // Check that salary figures appear in the report (formatted with commas or raw)
+    AssertTrue(report.Contains("90,000.00") || report.Contains("90000"), "Report: avg salary for Eng");
+// TEST GROUP 5: File I/O & Pipeline Integration (RED → GREEN)
+// Test: ParseFile reads sample CSV from disk
AssertEqual(10, employees.Count, "ParseFile: reads 10 rows from test_data.csv");
-// Test: ParseFile throws on missing file
+// Test: ParseFile throws FileNotFoundException for missing file
-    AssertTrue(threw, "ParseFile: throws FileNotFoundException on missing file");
// Test: Full pipeline — CSV → filter → aggregate → report file
+    // test_data.csv has 8 active employees (David Brown & Grace Lee are inactive)
-    // test_data.csv has 8 active employees (David Brown and Grace Lee are inactive)
-// Test: Pipeline with all-inactive data throws meaningful error
+// Test: Pipeline with all-inactive data throws
-    AssertTrue(threw, "Pipeline: throws when no active employees");
-// LIBRARY CODE (types must come after top-level statements in C#)
+// IMPLEMENTATION — types must follow top-level statements in C# file-based apps
+/// <summary>A single employee row from the CSV.</summary>
-// --- CSV Parser: converts CSV text or files into Employee records ---
+/// <summary>Parses CSV text or files into Employee records.</summary>
-    /// <summary>Parse CSV content from a string. First row must be the header.</summary>
+    /// <summary>Parse CSV from a string. First line must be the header row.</summary>
-        if (lines.Length < 2) return employees; // header only or empty

+/// <summary>Orchestrates: read CSV → filter active → aggregate → write report.</summary>
public static void Run(string inputCsvPath, string outputReportPath)

content.text:
Everything works. Here's a summary:

**Files created:**
- **tests.cs** — Self-contained test runner with 52 tests across 5 groups (CSV parsing, filtering, aggregation, report formatting, file I/O/pipeline). All pass.
- **app.cs** — Standalone CLI app: `dotnet run app.cs <input.csv> [output.txt]`
- **test_data.csv** — 10 employee records (8 active, 2 inactive) across 3 departments
- **report.txt** — Generated output showing 8 active employees, department summaries, and overall statistics

**Architecture:**
- `CsvParser` — parses CSV strings/files into `Employee` records with validation
- `EmployeeFilter` — filters to active employees (case-insensitive)
- `Aggregator` — computes avg salary/headcount by department + overall stats
- `ReportFormatter` — produces formatted text report
- `ReportPipeline` — orchestrates the full flow with error handling

result:
Everything works. Here's a summary:

**Files created:**
- **tests.cs** — Self-contained test runner with 52 tests across 5 groups (CSV parsing, filtering, aggregation, report formatting, file I/O/pipeline). All pass.
- **app.cs** — Standalone CLI app: `dotnet run app.cs <input.csv> [output.txt]`
- **test_data.csv** — 10 employee records (8 active, 2 inactive) across 3 departments
- **report.txt** — Generated output showing 8 active employees, department summaries, and overall statistics

**Architecture:**
- `CsvParser` — parses CSV strings/files into `Employee` records with validation
- `EmployeeFilter` — filters to active employees (case-insensitive)
- `Aggregator` — computes avg salary/headcount by department + overall stats
- `ReportFormatter` — produces formatted text report
- `ReportPipeline` — orchestrates the full flow with error handling
